title: Pisum nissolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called Pisum nissolia. However, I found that Pisum sativum, which is also known as the garden pea, has a close relative called Lathyrus nissolia, also known as the Nisolia pea.

Here's a paragraph about Lathyrus nissolia:

Lathyrus nissolia, also known as the Nisolia pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus nissolia include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal plant.
taxonomy: Pisum nissolia is a species within the genus Pisum, which is a group of annual legume plants in the family Fabaceae. The Pisum nissolia is commonly classified as a member of the tribe Fabeae and the order Fabales. This classification places it alongside other vetches and beans. Further, Pisum nissolia is often placed in the subfamily Faboideae. The combination of these classifications highlights the plant's position within the broader Fabaceae family.
